# AgenticGov — Making Agentic AI Work for Government

> A visual, no-login demonstration of the World Economic Forum (WEF) Readiness Framework for agentic AI in government. Six interconnected agentic applications spanning 70 government functions, wired into a single orchestration portal with cross-app event chains.

AgenticGov is a practical reference implementation of the WEF's *Making Agentic AI Work for Government* framework. Rather than describing what agentic AI in government could look like, it shows it: six purpose-built agentic applications that share state through a unified orchestration portal, with realistic cross-app event chains that mirror how interlocking government systems actually behave.

It is open to the public, requires no authentication, and is intended as a teaching tool, a sales-enablement walkthrough for government leaders, and a starting point for serious procurement conversations.

Built by On The Ground (https://ontheground.agency), a Singapore-based AI consulting and app development agency.

## The six agentic applications

Each app demonstrates one cluster of government functions. The apps share data through a unified portal so events propagate — for example, a permit issued in one app can trigger downstream actions in another.

Together the six apps cover **70+ government functions** across:

- Citizen services and case management
- Licensing, permits, and compliance
- Inspections and enforcement
- Benefits, eligibility, and entitlement
- Revenue and payments
- Procurement and grants
- Records, registries, and identity
- Inter-agency coordination and orchestration

## The WEF Readiness Framework

AgenticGov is built around the WEF's readiness dimensions for deploying agentic AI in government:

- **Mission alignment** — each agent has a clearly scoped purpose tied to a government function
- **Human-in-the-loop** — explicit checkpoints for officer review and override
- **Auditability** — traceable agent actions, decisions, and inputs
- **Inter-agent orchestration** — the cross-app event chain pattern
- **Citizen-facing safeguards** — transparency, recourse, and explainability

## Surfaces

- [Live demo](https://agenticgov.vercel.app): no login required
- [Orchestration portal](https://agenticgov.vercel.app): the unified entry point that wires the six apps together

## Audience

- Government leaders, ministers, and senior civil servants exploring agentic AI
- Digital-government and gov-tech practitioners
- Procurement and innovation officers
- WEF Readiness Framework readers wanting a working visual companion
- Vendors and partners scoping serious agentic-AI deployments
- Researchers studying agentic AI in public-sector contexts

## How it is built

- React 19 + Vite (TypeScript) frontend
- Tailwind CSS v4
- Node.js / Vercel Functions backend
- Neon Postgres (optional, used for visitor counter only)
- Deployed on Vercel
- No authentication required — the demo is public by design

## What it is not

- Not a production government system. It is a reference implementation and walkthrough.
- Not a closed-source product. The architecture pattern (six agents + orchestration portal + event chain) is the deliverable.

## About the build partner

On The Ground is a Singapore-based AI consulting and app development agency that builds practical, AI-powered web applications for SMEs, professional service firms, government and civic organisations, and social-impact organisations. We ship in 1-week sprints. Founded July 2023.
